page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It does not feature local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8gn.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in Passmark memory testing. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 285.68 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 134.17 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark database operations also show strong performance. However, the instance exhibits limitations in machine learning tasks, with poor text generation speeds of 42.44 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models and 2.39 tokens/sec on large 70B models.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and is optimized for EBS and network-heavy operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture delivers high memory density and strong memory throughput without the premium associated with x86 hardware. This profile makes the r8gn.4xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, data buffering, and high-throughput network applications, whereas it is less suited for heavy LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth at 285.68 GB/sec and strong database operation capabilities. Conversely, it shows weak performance in LLM text generation tasks. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its high resource density and ARM architecture. It is best suited for databases, caching systems, and network-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, 50 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server with 16 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 server delivering high memory bandwidth and dedicated core performance for database and network-heavy workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Top-tier memory performance with 285.68 GB/sec large-block read bandwidth
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
